title: 26 By Biosemi
description: This EEG dataset comprises neurophysiological recordings from participants undergoing laser-evoked pain stimulation with concurrent pain intensity ratings. Participants first underwent a calibration phase with laser stimulations of varying intensities to determine the energy level corresponding to an average pain rating of 7/10. Subsequently, each participant received fixed-intensity laser stimulation approximately every 20 seconds for 16 trials while providing subjective pain assessments on a 0-10 scale. The dataset captures brain electrical activity during pain perception and rating tasks, providing insights into the neural correlates of pain processing and subjective pain experience.
methods_description: Participants underwent an initial calibration phase where the experimenter determined the laser energy intensity corresponding to an average pain rating of 7/10 across the group. Subsequently, each participant received fixed-intensity laser stimulation approximately every 20 seconds for a total of 16 trials. Following each stimulus, there was a 3-5 second pause, after which participants orally rated pain intensity (0-10 scale) within a 3-5 second window. The subsequent trial commenced randomly within 1-3 seconds after ratings were provided. Participants maintained fixation on a visual cross throughout the experiment with eyes open.
